title: Iranian President Claims US Military Bases Enable Iran to Target Regional Territories
summary: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian declared on Thursday that US military bases in the region constitute legitimate targets for Iran, arguing that foreign military presence endangers host countries and destabilizes the region. Pezeshkian attributed any insecurity in the Strait of Hormuz to US and Israeli actions.
contents:
Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian delivered remarks on Thursday, April 30, 2026, in which he asserted that the presence of US military bases in the region creates justification for treating those territories as legitimate military targets.

Pezeshkian argued that foreign military presence in the region endangers host countries rather than enhancing security. The president contended that the stationing of US forces increases regional tensions and destabilizes the area, contrary to stated security objectives.

The Iranian leader further blamed the United States and Israel for any insecurity affecting the Strait of Hormuz, a vital international shipping corridor. His comments reflected escalating rhetoric amid the broader Middle East crisis and highlighted the role of great power military deployments in regional tensions.
